data.title: Raffles Interior Faces Shareholder-Led Board Shake-Up Amid Trading Suspension
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Raffles Interior Ltd. ( (HK:1376) ).

Raffles Interior Limited has received a requisition notice from a group of shareholders holding about 10.5% of its shares, demanding an extraordinary general meeting to overhaul the board. The proposed resolutions seek the removal of multiple existing executive and independent non-executive directors and the appointment of a new slate of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.

The move follows earlier complaints and disputes involving alleged special dealings and disclosure issues, and comes while trading in the company’s shares remains suspended. The requisition underscores heightened governance tensions and could significantly reshape the company’s leadership structure, with potential implications for its strategic direction and stakeholder confidence.

More about Raffles Interior Ltd.

Raffles Interior Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the interior solutions and fit-out sector. Its shares trade on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and it has an international shareholder base actively engaged in corporate governance matters.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$96M

data.title: IMAX China Posts Lower First-Half 2026 Profit Amid Revenue Decline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from IMAX China Holding ( (HK:1970) ) is now available.

IMAX China Holding, Inc., a leading provider of premium large-format cinema technologies and related services in Greater China, reported unaudited interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2026. The company focuses on advanced projection systems and theatrical services, supporting high-end movie exhibition across the region.

For the first half of 2026, IMAX China’s revenues fell to US$34.4 million from US$57.8 million a year earlier, with gross profit down to US$18.4 million from US$40.1 million, reflecting a weaker top line despite maintaining profitability. Net profit attributable to shareholders dropped to US$8.1 million from US$23.9 million, while total comprehensive income was US$16.3 million, aided by favorable foreign currency translation. The results, reviewed by PwC with an unmodified report, highlight pressure on operating performance ahead of the company’s interim report to shareholders.

The announcement also notes that controlling shareholder IMAX Corporation released its unaudited second-quarter and first-half 2026 results, underscoring the close linkage between the parent’s global operations and IMAX China’s regional business. Stakeholders will read the softer earnings as a signal of current challenges in the cinema and entertainment market, even as the group remains profitable and continues to benefit from its premium positioning.

More about IMAX China Holding

IMAX China Holding, Inc. operates in the premium cinema technology and entertainment industry, focusing on large-format projection systems and related services across Greater China. The company derives revenue from exhibition technology, content distribution and associated services, positioning itself as a key player in high-end moviegoing experiences in the region.

As a subsidiary of IMAX Corporation, IMAX China benefits from its parent’s global brand and technology platform while concentrating on the Chinese and Asian markets. Its business performance is closely tied to cinema attendance, film release schedules and broader trends in the entertainment and media sectors.

Average Trading Volume: 144,597

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$2.79B

The latest announcement is out from MOG Holdings Limited ( (HK:1942) ).

MOG Digitech Holdings Limited has announced a further delay in sending out the prospectus for its proposed rights issue, which offers one rights share for every two existing shares held on the record date on a non-underwritten basis. The prospectus, originally expected by 24 July 2026, is now scheduled for despatch on or before 28 July 2026 as the company requires additional time to prepare and finalise the information.

The company has released a revised timetable for the rights issue and associated placing, setting new dates for trading of nil-paid rights shares, acceptance and payment deadlines, commencement and long stop date of placing, and the start of trading in fully-paid rights shares in early September 2026. These changes affect the timeline for shareholders’ participation, the handling of unsubscribed rights shares, and the completion of capital-raising steps, but do not alter the core terms of the rights issue itself.

More about MOG Holdings Limited

MOG Digitech Holdings Limited, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the technology sector and is incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability. The company serves shareholders in the public capital markets and is currently pursuing a rights issue to raise equity capital on a non-underwritten basis.

Its market activities are focused on engaging existing shareholders through structured corporate actions, offering new shares in proportion to current holdings and managing related placing arrangements to optimise participation and allocation.

Natural Food International Holding Ltd.’s controlling shareholder, Natural Capital, has increased its stake in the company by purchasing 2,264,000 shares on the open market between 3 July and 23 July 2026. An update from Life Healthcare Group ( (HK:0928) ) is now available.

King International Investment Limited plans to consolidate every ten existing shares of HK$0.05 into one consolidated share of HK$0.50, leaving authorised share capital unchanged at HK$500 million but cutting issued shares to about 185 million. The board also intends to reduce the trading board lot size from 20,000 existing shares to 5,000 consolidated shares once the share consolidation takes effect.

Conditional on the consolidation, the company will launch a non-underwritten rights issue of two rights shares for every one consolidated share at HK$0.50 per share to raise up to about HK$193.6 million before expenses. Net proceeds are estimated at between HK$183.8 million and HK$192.4 million, depending on option exercises, and are earmarked to strengthen the company’s capital base and support its stated strategic and operational needs.

data.title: EDA Group Expands UK Logistics Footprint with Major West Midlands Lease
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

EDA Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2505) ) has provided an update.

EDA Group Holdings Limited has secured a major logistics facility in the U.K. through its subsidiary EDA Cloud UK Ltd, which has entered into a long-term lease for a 324,366 square foot warehouse and office premises in the Citadel Logistics Centre in Wednesbury, West Midlands. The site will be used for warehouse storage, distribution and ancillary office operations, supporting the group’s expansion in logistics and cloud-enabled services.

The ten-year lease, classified as a major transaction under Hong Kong listing rules due to the size of the right-of-use asset, received written approval from shareholders holding more than half of the company’s voting rights. The agreement features a stepped rent structure with initial discounted rent, subsequent increases, and CPI-linked rent reviews, indicating a material, long-term commitment that enhances EDA Group’s operational capacity and presence in a key U.K. logistics corridor, with implications for its cost base, scalability and market positioning.

The structured rent concessions and review mechanisms suggest the company has negotiated terms to balance near-term investment in capacity with longer-term cost predictability. For stakeholders, this signals confidence in sustained demand for EDA Group’s logistics and cloud services, as the enlarged U.K. footprint is expected to underpin growth and potentially strengthen its competitive stance in international distribution and infrastructure services.

More about EDA Group Holdings Limited

EDA Group Holdings Limited, through its wholly owned subsidiary EDA Cloud UK Ltd, operates in the logistics and cloud-related services sector with a focus on warehouse storage, distribution and associated office uses. The group is expanding its physical footprint in the United Kingdom, targeting a large-scale logistics hub in the West Midlands to support its operational growth and service capabilities.

The company’s activities indicate a strategic emphasis on enhancing infrastructure to support cloud and logistics operations, leveraging modern warehouse facilities to improve efficiency and regional coverage. Its decision to secure a long-term lease in a major UK logistics centre underscores a commitment to serving clients that rely on robust storage and distribution networks.

By strengthening its presence in the UK, EDA Group positions itself to capture demand from businesses requiring integrated logistics and technology-backed distribution solutions. This move suggests a broader strategy to build scalable, international operations anchored in key transport and industrial corridors.

Average Trading Volume: 361,678

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Shanghai Kindly Medical Instruments Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1501) ) has shared an update.

Shanghai INT Medical Instruments has agreed, via a wholly owned subsidiary, to acquire 100% of the partnership interests in Shanghai Maoyi for US$55.1 million, which indirectly holds 579,866 series B preferred shares in a target company, equal to about 3.85% of its issued share capital. data.title: China Nature Energy Says ICAC Probe of Chairman Not Linked to Company Affairs
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Nature Energy Technology Holdings Limited ( (HK:1597) ) has shared an announcement.

China Nature Energy Technology Holdings Limited, a Cayman-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, is led by chairman and executive director Mr. Cheng Liquan Richard alongside a board of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. While Mr. Cheng oversees day-to-day operations and general management, the execution of production and operational matters is jointly managed by other executives and the wider management team.

The company announced that Mr. Cheng has been invited by Hong Kong’s Independent Commission Against Corruption to assist in an investigation related solely to his other personal businesses, and he has not been charged with any offence. The board stated that, based on information currently available and the preliminary nature of the probe, it believes the investigation has no material adverse impact on the group’s affairs and sees no reason to consider Mr. Cheng unsuitable to continue as chairman and executive director, while committing to monitor developments and update shareholders in line with listing rules.

The announcement underscores the board’s effort to reassure investors and other stakeholders that the investigation is not connected to the group’s operations and that its distributed management structure mitigates operational risk. By affirming continuity in leadership and pledging further disclosures as necessary, the company aims to maintain regulatory compliance and market confidence pending any future developments in the case.

More about China Nature Energy Technology Holdings Limited

China Nature Energy Technology Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1597, operates through a group structure overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. The chairman and executive director is Mr. Cheng Liquan Richard, with day-to-day production and operational execution jointly handled by other executives and management.

The group’s governance framework emphasizes a separation between overall management oversight and operational execution, with multiple directors sharing responsibility. This structure underpins the board’s assessment of potential risks to the company’s operations when issues arise involving individual directors, including regulatory or investigative developments.

The company is subject to the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited, requiring disclosure of certain matters concerning directors. Its regulatory reporting practices include announcements under specific listing rules when events may be relevant to shareholders’ assessment of director suitability or potential impacts on corporate affairs.

Average Trading Volume: 63,827

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1.91B

data.title: Continental Aerospace Technologies Clears Key Hurdle in Asset Disposal and Delisting Plan
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Ltd. ( (HK:0232) ) just unveiled an update.

Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Ltd. has advanced its proposed disposal of the entire issued share capital of Motto Investment Limited, receiving clearance from the German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy. This satisfies one of the key regulatory conditions under its sale and purchase agreement with MOBILE ACQUISITIONCO, LLC, moving the transaction closer to completion.

Several other conditions precedent, including shareholder approvals, remain outstanding or capable of waiver, and closing will only occur after all are resolved. The company has obtained an extension to late July for issuing its circular to shareholders, and it reiterates that the disposal, special dividend, property sale and planned delisting and winding-up may or may not proceed, with the final dividend amount dependent on the ultimate disposal consideration.

These proposals, if completed, would result in a very substantial restructuring of Continental Aerospace Technologies, including a withdrawal of its listing and distribution of cash to shareholders. Stakeholders face significant changes to their investment as the company transitions away from its current public-market status, with regulatory clearances and formal approvals determining the final outcome.

More about Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Ltd.

Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Ltd. is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the aerospace sector through its ownership of Motto Investment Limited and related assets. The group focuses on aviation technologies and associated property holdings, and is undergoing a strategic restructuring that includes a proposed withdrawal from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

MOBILE ACQUISITIONCO, LLC, established in Delaware, is the purchaser involved in the planned acquisition of Continental Aerospace Technologies’ core operating assets, positioning itself as the new owner of Motto Investment Limited subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals.

The company’s current corporate actions highlight a transition phase that will materially change its listed status and capital structure, with implications for existing shareholders and its presence in public markets.

Average Trading Volume: 81,723,388

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Rimbaco Group Global Limited has announced that Hong Kong’s stock exchange granted it a temporary waiver from the minimum public float requirement following the close of a takeover offer by its controlling shareholder Aureole Halo Limited. Jinshang Bank Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2558) ) has shared an announcement.

Jinshang Bank has detailed the pricing basis for its RMB730 million capital contribution to Jinshang Xiaojin, under which it will subscribe 500 million new shares at RMB1.46 per share. The valuation, mandated by state-owned asset rules and approved by Jinshang Xiaojin’s shareholders, lifts the consumer finance company’s fair value to about RMB1.461 billion and will give Jinshang Bank a 70% stake.

The bank said the move is driven by regulatory requirements for consumer finance firms to raise minimum registered capital to at least RMB1 billion and to ensure a major shareholder holds at least 50%. Management highlighted that Jinshang Xiaojin’s nationwide consumer finance license and asset growth in the sector enhance the strategic fit with the bank’s existing operations, reinforcing its position in a growing market while addressing ongoing regulatory rectification demands.

More about Jinshang Bank Co., Ltd. Class H

Jinshang Bank Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated joint stock commercial bank whose operations include retail and corporate banking services. Through its subsidiary Jinshang Xiaojin, the bank participates in the consumer finance sector, focusing on nationwide consumer lending and leveraging a rare province-based license that allows it to operate across China.

Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) has issued an update.

Yellowstone International Limited, managed by Argyle Street Management, has raised its voluntary cash partial offer for up to 230 million shares of Greentech Technology International Limited from HK$0.40 to HK$0.55 per share. The revised price represents a significant premium to the last trading price and keeps all other offer terms unchanged, underscoring a stronger bid to entice existing shareholders.

At the new offer price, the total potential consideration rises to HK$126.5 million, which the offeror will fund entirely from internal resources, with its financial adviser confirming sufficient liquidity. The premium of roughly 96% over the last trading day’s closing price signals a more aggressive stance by Yellowstone, potentially reshaping Greentech’s ownership structure and highlighting the company’s perceived strategic value in the market.

More about Greentech Technology International Limited

Greentech Technology International Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company whose shares are the subject of an unsolicited partial takeover offer, indicating it operates in a sector attracting strategic investment interest. Its shareholder base includes qualifying shareholders targeted by Yellowstone International Limited, which is pursuing a cash-based acquisition strategy through a managed offer structure.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: China Shenhua Board Backs New Independent Directors in Governance-Focused Move
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

China Shenhua Energy Co ( (HK:1088) ) has issued an update.

China Shenhua Energy Company Limited has released an overseas regulatory announcement summarizing resolutions passed at the 20th meeting of the sixth session of its board of directors, held in Beijing on 23 July 2026. The board meeting complied with PRC company law, applicable listing rules and the company’s Articles of Association, with directors attending in person, by proxy and via video connection.

At the meeting, the board approved a proposal to nominate Yih, Dieter Lai Tak and Zhang Mengjiao as candidates for independent non-executive directors for the remainder of the current board term. The nomination committee concluded that the candidates meet legal and regulatory requirements, have relevant experience, and satisfy corporate governance standards, and the board agreed to submit the proposal to the general meeting for shareholder consideration.

The resolutions underline China Shenhua’s ongoing refreshment of its independent oversight and adherence to governance norms for listed companies in China and Hong Kong. For stakeholders, the appointment of qualified independent non-executive directors is intended to reinforce board independence, regulatory compliance and oversight quality as the company manages its large integrated energy operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1088) stock is a Hold
with a HK$36.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Shenhua Energy Co stock,
see the HK:1088 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Shenhua Energy Co

China Shenhua Energy Company Limited is a leading integrated energy company in the People’s Republic of China, listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai. The group operates across coal production, power generation, and related energy services, serving domestic and international markets as one of China’s largest coal-based energy suppliers.

The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive, independent non-executive and employee directors, and is subject to PRC company law and listing rules in both Hong Kong and Shanghai. Its corporate governance framework follows the Articles of Association and relevant codes for listed companies.
